name: Dive Bar

interaction_intensity.label: Low Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: Minimal expectation to perform. Solo patrons are common and usually left to their own devices.

social_dynamics: Regulars at the bar, low expectations, easy conversation
solo_tips: The bar-end seats are the most 'invisible' spots.
Bartenders are usually happy to chat if it's a slow weeknight.

safety_factors.label: Moderate Risk
safety_factors.description: Moderate due to environmental hazards: dim lighting hides floor spills and regulars can be unpredictable.

physical_proximity: Spacious at tables; shoulder-to-shoulder at the bar rail.
recommendations: Check your seat for stability before sitting
Keep your phone handy for dim areas

name: Irish Pub

interaction_intensity.label: Low Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: Very welcoming; the 'living room' vibe makes it socially acceptable to sit alone for long periods.

social_dynamics: Communal but respectful; easy to stay anonymous or join a conversation at the bar.
solo_tips: Great for a solo pint and a book.
Sit at the bar rail near the service well to be social without 'trying.'

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Generally very safe and communal; risk is mostly related to over-drinking due to the 'homely' atmosphere.

physical_proximity: Varies; booths offer high personal space, while communal tables involve close contact.
recommendations: Booth seating is best for solo privacy
Communal tables are the 'social' green light

name: Sports Bar

interaction_intensity.label: Low-Moderate Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: Pressure is tied to the game; as long as you're watching the screen, no one questions you being solo.

social_dynamics: Group cheering/groaning; high potential for 'incidental' conversation about the game.
solo_tips: The bar rail is the prime spot; looking at the TV is the perfect social 'shield'.
Wear a jersey if you want people to strike up a conversation.

safety_factors.label: Moderate Risk
safety_factors.description: Can become high-intensity if the game is high-stakes; alcohol-fueled emotions run high.

physical_proximity: Close quarters during big plays; otherwise standard table spacing.
recommendations: Arrive 30 minutes before kickoff to snag a bar seat
Be aware of 'rival' fans if the atmosphere gets heated.

name: Hi-Fi Listening Bar

interaction_intensity.label: Very Low Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: The music is the primary guest. Talking over the records is often discouraged, making solo presence the norm.

social_dynamics: Individual appreciation of sound; hushed tones; respect for the vinyl curator.
solo_tips: Bring a book or journal; it fits the 'contemplative' aesthetic perfectly.
Seats facing the speakers offer the best audio immersion.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Generally controlled environments with attentive staff and predictable crowd behavior.

recommendations: Check the 'listening rules' if posted at the entrance

name: Wine Tasting / Brewery Tour

interaction_intensity.label: Structured Socializing
interaction_intensity.description: Interaction is often guided by a host or sommelier, providing easy 'scripts' for talking to neighbors.

social_dynamics: Shared sensory experience; focus on craft and origin; polite and paced conversation.
solo_tips: Sit at the tasting bar rather than a table; the staff will keep you engaged with 'tasting notes'.
Ask technical questions about the fermentation—it’s the easiest icebreaker.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Usually high-end or professional environments with a focus on 'sipping' rather than 'chugging'.

physical_proximity: Comfortable; usually involves dedicated personal space at a table or bar rail.
recommendations: Use the 'dump bucket' if you are driving

name: Indian / Telugu Sports Bar

interaction_intensity.label: High Communal Energy
interaction_intensity.description: Interaction peaks during live matches (Cricket/IPL). Expect high-volume cheering and table-to-table banter.

social_dynamics: Strong emphasis on regional pride and shared sports fandom; feels like a community living room.
solo_tips: Wear a team jersey (like SRH or India) to immediately gain 'regular' status.
The bar counter is usually quieter for solo dining while still catching the screen.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Generally very welcoming and family-friendly environments.

physical_proximity: High density during major matches; communal seating is common.
recommendations: Check match times before heading out
Try the small-plate appetizers to share the 'snack culture'

name: Barcade

interaction_intensity.label: Activity-Based
interaction_intensity.description: Most people are focused on the screens. Interaction is usually brief, like 'Who has next?' or 'Nice high score!'

social_dynamics: Low-stakes competition; shared nostalgia; easy to start/stop social engagement.
solo_tips: Look for single-player cabinet games if you want to be left alone; pinball is the ultimate solo barcade sport.
Keep a drink holder nearby so you don't leave your glass unattended while playing.

safety_factors.label: Low-Moderate Risk
safety_factors.description: Generally safe, but high-energy movements (like Skee-Ball or DDR) can lead to accidental bumps.

recommendations: Carry a few five-dollar bills for the token machine
Wipe down the controls before/after use

name: Latin Bar

interaction_intensity.label: Active Engagement
interaction_intensity.description: More 'club' than 'bar'—expect to be asked to dance or to be in a high-density social environment.

social_dynamics: Highly social; frequent approaching; energy often stays high until very late.
solo_tips: Leaning against the bar with a drink makes you approachable without looking 'lost'.
Learning basic reggaeton 'step-touches' helps you blend in without needing a partner.

safety_factors.label: Moderate Risk
safety_factors.description: Can get very crowded; watch for floor spills in the high-traffic zones.

physical_proximity: High; constant movement and close-quarters dancing.
recommendations: Check if there is a cover charge before you arrive
Arrive after 11 PM for the peak vibe
